elements.title: Marie Engle
elements.contents: ... that Marie Engle (pictured) was one of the earliest operatic singers to be recorded on rare Bettini cylinders?

elements.title: Enrei Onodachi Memorial Festival
elements.contents: ... that the Enrei Onodachi Memorial Festival can last for only 15 seconds?

elements.title: The Tigress of Forlì
elements.contents: ... that The Tigress of Forlì earned its title because its protagonist was accused of being "willing to eat her young to gain power"?

elements.title: a California radio station
elements.contents: ... that a woman died of water intoxication after participating in a contest held by a California radio station?

elements.title: an 1887 book on British whisky distilleries
elements.contents: ... that the author of an 1887 book on British whisky distilleries didn't know how to distill whisky?

elements.title: Trimithis
elements.contents: ... that a wealthy councillor's house in the ancient Egyptian desert city Trimithis preserves a line from a lost tragedy by Euripides?

elements.title: Yo tenía diez perritos
elements.contents: ... that counting songs such as "Yo tenía diez perritos" were once thought to have been used as chants for curing illnesses?

elements.title: The Laffer Utilities
elements.contents: ... that the "nonproductivity" office software The Laffer Utilities was created as a spin-off of the adult-themed franchise Leisure Suit Larry?

elements.title: Colleen McCullough
elements.contents: ... that Colleen McCullough described the television adaptation of her best-known novel as "instant vomit"?
